title: Two-seed RealMLP with low-weight CatBoost correction
summary: Blend the two-seed RealMLP core with a CatBoost expert using only a small CatBoost weight.
rationale: CatBoost-only showed a confirmed positive signal, but broad XGBoost/CatBoost averaging was negative. This patch treats CatBoost as a small residual corrector rather than an equal peer expert.
implementation_hint: Start from the parent two-seed RealMLP average code. Add one CatBoostClassifier trained on the same folds and compatible features. Use GPU when available with task_type='GPU' and devices='0'. Blend final probabilities using a small OOF-selected grid such as RealMLP weight 0.90, 0.925, and 0.95. Do not add XGBoost, LightGBM, logistic stacking, calibration, or feature changes.
expected_effect: Improve OOF AUC if CatBoost captures a small complementary residual signal.
risk: CatBoost may be correlated or noisy enough that even a small weight reduces the strong RealMLP score.
